title: Framework Pulse Relay
description: Patch a collapsing networked A-frame framework by tapping glowing frame joints back in pulse order before the signal drops.

howToPlayJson.title: Framework Pulse Relay
howToPlayJson.objective: Memorize each broadcast pulse route across linked A-frames, then replay the exact joint sequence to keep the framework online and build the highest combo before three link failures end the run.

controls.input: Tap the highlighted frame joints in remembered order
controls.outcome: Correct taps rebuild the route and extend the combo

controls.input: When a frame shows a flip icon, tap that frame's last flashed joint first
controls.outcome: You satisfy the temporary reverse-order rule and keep the multiplier alive

controls.input: Press and hold the center core between rounds
controls.outcome: You lock in part of your combo for safety but lose a little timer time

steps: A short pulse melody travels through 3 linked A-frames, lighting specific joints in sequence.
The player replays the route by tapping the same joints in order on a mobile-friendly single screen.
Every 2 rounds, one frame hot-swaps into a readable rule state for a few seconds: mirrored frames must be replayed in reverse only for that
Longer routes, faster pulse playback, and extra connected frames appear as the session progresses toward a 2-4 minute failure point.
A wrong tap or timeout causes a link failure, breaks the current combo, and removes one of three remaining framework shields.
The run ends at zero shields, then the score is posted to a simple local-first daily leaderboard with optional online sync when available.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score 10 points per correct joint, +25 per cleared route, and a combo multiplier that rises by 0.5x each flawless round up to 5x. Banking streaks preserves half the multiplier after the next mistake. Leaderboards rank by
tips: Watch the pulse melody as much as the light path; each joint has its own audio cue.
Use the brief slow-motion just before replay to chunk the route into frame-sized groups.
Bank a streak before a 6-plus step route if you are carrying a high multiplier.
When a frame flips, only that frame's local order reverses, not the full route.
